title: Feeling Good
subtitle: The New Mood Therapy
author: David D. Burns
themes: Your thoughts create your feelings — change the thoughts, change the mood
Cognitive distortions are systematic thinking errors that fuel depression
CBT is as effective as medication for mild to moderate depression
Specific techniques can counter each cognitive distortion
Self-help based on CBT principles can produce lasting change
excerpt: The paradox of depression is that your feelings are not trustworthy. When you are depressed, your mind plays tricks on you. Everything looks bleak, hopeless, and overwhelming. But this dark vision is nearly always distorted. The cognitive therapy approach I describe in this book will teach you to recognize the distortions in your thinking and replace them with more realistic, balanced thoughts.
